paper_title: Experimental Procedures for Sediment and Molecular Analysis

methods.method_name: Sediment Core Collection and Pore Water Analysis
methods.description: Collection of sediment cores from thermokarst lakes and analysis of pore water to study sediment composition and chemical properties.

steps.description: Sediment cores were collected from two nearby thermokarst lakes using a hand-operated Uwitec gravity coring system.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment cores using gravity coring system

steps.description: Pore-water samples were obtained from cores at a 10 cm resolution with ceramic rhizons and treated with potassium iodide.
steps.searchable_summary: extract and treat pore-water samples from sediment cores

steps.description: Pore water data was analyzed by ICP-OES for various elements and by CFA for specific ions.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze pore water using ICP-OES and CFA

methods.description: Analysis of gas samples to measure concentrations of CH4, H2, CO2, and O2 using gas chromatography.

steps.description: Gas samples were withdrawn with a gas-tight syringe and injected into a gas chromatograph.
steps.searchable_summary: inject gas samples into gas chromatograph

steps.description: Concentration of gases was calculated by the sum of the gas concentration in the headspace and liquid phase.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate gas concentrations from chromatograph data

steps.description: Maximum production and consumption rates of CH4 were calculated.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate CH4 production and consumption rates

methods.description: Isolation of DNA from sediment samples and analysis of microbial diversity using 16S rRNA gene sequencing.

steps.description: DNA was extracted using the PowerSoil DNA Isolation Kit with modifications.
16S rRNA gene amplicon sequencing was performed to assess microbial diversity.
Sequences were filtered, checked for chimeras, clustered into OTUs, and classified.
